note: DAST is not applicable for training_playground (no live server to probe). All other categories have demo findings from known-bad training files.

findings.description: [B602] subprocess_popen_with_shell_equals_true: subprocess call with shell=True identified, security issue.

ai_triage.suggested_fix: Replace shell=True with a list of arguments: subprocess.Popen(['echo', user_input])

findings.description: [B301] pickle: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

ai_triage.suggested_fix: Use json.loads() for trusted data, or hmac-signed payloads for untrusted input.

findings.description: [TRAINING DEMO] requests==2.28.1 is pinned below the fix for CVE-2023-32681 (proxy auth header leak). Upgrade to >=2.31.0. urllib3<1.26.17 is also below the fix for CVE-2023-43804.

ai_triage.suggested_fix: Upgrade: requests>=2.31.0 and urllib3>=1.26.17 in requirements.txt

findings.description: [TRAINING DEMO] Hardcoded AWS Access Key ID pattern detected (AKIA4HGEXAMPLEFAKE99 at line 11). In production: run `gitleaks detect` or `trufflehog git` against the repo.

ai_triage.suggested_fix: Never hardcode credentials. Use environment variables: aws_secret = os.environ['AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY']

findings.description: [TRAINING DEMO] S3 bucket 'insecure' has no public access block enabled. In production: run trivy config or checkov -d . against your Terraform.

ai_triage.suggested_fix: Add an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block resource with block_public_acls=true, block_public_policy=true, ignore_public_acls=true, restrict_public_buckets=true.

findings.description: [TRAINING DEMO] Unencrypted PII pattern in notebook cell outputs. In production: run nbdefense scan against your notebooks.

ai_triage.suggested_fix: Strip outputs before committing: jupyter nbconvert --clear-output --inplace notebook.ipynb
